name: Command Center Dashboard
title: Command Center Dashboard
description: Personal dashboard showing upcoming calendar events, open GitHub PRs awaiting review, and recent Slack messages needing attention. Auto-refreshes every 30 minutes during business hours.
prompt:
## Command Center Dashboard

### What It Does
A personal productivity dashboard at https://command-center.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app showing upcoming calendar events, open GitHub PRs awaiting review, and recent Slack messages — all on one page with a draggable GridStack layout.

### Architecture
- **Tech stack:** Static HTML + vanilla JS, no build step. Uses GridStack 10 for draggable/resizable widget grid.
- **File structure:**
  - `apps/command-center/index.html` — main dashboard page
  - `apps/command-center/db.js` — SyncDB schema config (CC_DB_CONFIG) defining all tables
  - `apps/command-center/data/calendar.json` — calendar events data
  - `apps/command-center/data/prs.json` — GitHub PR data
  - `apps/command-center/data/slack.json` — Slack messages data
- **Shared components:** `<cc-topbar>`, `<cc-nav active="dashboard">`, `<cc-auth>`, `<cc-toast>`
- **Shared modules:** `<cc-calendar>`, `<cc-prs>`, `<cc-slack>`, plus summary modules for ideas, client health, contentful, leads
- **SyncDB:** Uses `sql-sync.js` for in-browser SQLite with IndexedDB caching and server-side JSON persistence

### Step-by-Step Build Instructions
1. Create `apps/command-center/` directory
2. Create `index.html` with:
   ```html
   <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://shared.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/theme.css">
   <link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/gridstack@10/dist/gridstack.min.css" rel="stylesheet">
   <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/gridstack@10/dist/gridstack-all.js"></script>
   <script src="https://shared.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/components/index.js"></script>
   <script src="https://shared.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/modules/index.js"></script>
   <script src="https://shared.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/sql-sync.js"></script>
   <script src="db.js"></script>
   ```
3. Add sticky header with `<cc-topbar>` and `<cc-nav active="dashboard">`
4. Add `<cc-auth>` and `<cc-toast>`
5. Add "My Apps" panel showing links to all deployed apps as card links
6. Add GridStack container with widget items: `<cc-calendar src="data/calendar.json">`, `<cc-prs src="data/prs.json">`, `<cc-slack src="data/slack.json">`, plus summary widgets
7. Add layout persistence in localStorage and a "Reset Layout" button
8. Create `db.js` defining CC_DB_CONFIG with tables for: ideas, recipes, clients, leads, media, calendar, prs, slack, crons, clientHealth, contentfulHealth, ga4Alerts, dryAudit, community

### Data Schema
- **calendar.json:** `[{id, title/summary, start, end, location, attendees[], status, description, htmlLink, isZoom, zoomLink}]`
- **prs.json:** `[{id, title, url, state, createdAt, updatedAt, labels[], author: {login, type}, repository: {name}}]`
- **slack.json:** `[{id, text, user/username, channel, timestamp/ts, reactions[], permalink}]`

### UI/UX Details
- Glass & Depth theme: `--bg: #0a0e1a`, `--accent: #f59e0b`, `--card: rgba(255,255,255,0.08)`, serif headings (Georgia), 16px border-radius, backdrop-filter blur
- GridStack widgets are draggable/resizable with drag handles that appear on hover
- My Apps section shows app cards with emoji icons in a flex row
- Page header shows last-updated timestamp

### Integration Points
- Data refreshed by the `refresh-command-center` cron (every 30min, 7AM-4PM PT weekdays)
- Calendar data from Google Calendar API, PRs from `gh` CLI, Slack from Slack search API

### Testing/Verification
- Visit https://command-center.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app
- Verify password gate appears, enter password
- Confirm calendar, PR, and Slack widgets load with data
- Drag widgets to rearrange, refresh page — layout should persist

name: Media Gallery
title: Media Gallery
description: Filterable gallery of all generated media — images, PDFs, presentations. Supports keyword search, tag filtering, and type filtering.
prompt:
## Media Gallery

### What It Does
A filterable gallery page at https://command-center.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/gallery.html displaying all generated media (images, PDFs, Gamma presentations) with search, type filtering, and tag filtering.

### Architecture
- **File structure:**
  - `apps/command-center/gallery.html` — gallery page
  - `apps/command-center/data/media.json` — media metadata
- **Shared components:** `<cc-topbar>`, `<cc-nav active="gallery">`, `<cc-auth>`, `<cc-toast>`
- **Shared module:** `<cc-gallery>` Web Component (`apps/shared/modules/cc-gallery.js`)
- **SyncDB table:** `media` — `id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, type TEXT, url TEXT, description TEXT, tags TEXT, createdAt TEXT` (tags is JSON array)

### Step-by-Step Build
1. Create `gallery.html`:
   ```html
   <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://shared.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/theme.css">
   <script src="https://shared.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/components/index.js"></script>
   <script src="https://shared.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/modules/index.js"></script>
   <script src="https://shared.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app/sql-sync.js"></script>
   <script src="db.js"></script>
   ```
2. Add `<cc-gallery src="data/media.json">` in body
3. Create `cc-gallery.js` module with:
   - Search input filtering by name
   - Type filter pills (All, Image, PDF, Presentation) — extracted from data
   - Tag pills with color-coded toggle (10 rotating colors)
   - Responsive grid of cards showing thumbnail (or icon for PDF/Presentation), name, type, tags, creation date
   - URL state persistence via `CC.getParams()`/`CC.setParams()`

### Data Schema
```json
{"id": "unique-id", "name": "Image Name", "type": "Image", "url": "https://...", "thumbnail": "https://...", "description": "...", "tags": ["client", "presentation"], "createdAt": "2026-01-15"}
```

### UI/UX Details
- Glass cards with hover shadow transition
- Tag pills have per-tag consistent colors from a 10-color palette
- Image thumbnails displayed inline; PDF/Presentation show Lucide icons
- Uses `.grid.grid-auto` CSS class for responsive columns
- Page header shows item count

### Testing
- Visit gallery.html, verify items display
- Test search, type filter, tag filter — all combinable
- Verify URL params update and survive page refresh

name: Morning Weather Briefing
title: Morning Weather Briefing
description: Daily weather forecast at 6 AM PT with hourly breakdown for home address.
prompt:
## Morning Weather Briefing

### What It Does
A daily cron job at 6:00 AM Pacific that sends an hourly weather forecast for Adam's home location via Slack DM. Includes temperature, precipitation chance, wind, and notable conditions.

### Architecture
- **Type:** OpenClaw scheduled cron job
- **Schedule:** `0 14 * * *` (6 AM PT = 14:00 UTC)
- **Skill used:** `weather` skill (skills/weather/)

### Setup Instructions
1. Create an OpenClaw cron job:
   - Name: `⛅ Morning Weather (6 AM PT)`
   - Schedule: `0 14 * * *`
   - Prompt: "Fetch the hourly weather forecast for today for Ridgewood, NJ. Include temperature, precipitation %, wind speed, and any notable conditions. Format as a clean Slack message with emoji. Deliver via Slack DM."

### Integration Points
- Uses the `weather` skill which calls a weather API
- Delivers results via Slack DM using the `message` tool
- Runs daily including weekends

### Testing
- Trigger manually: tell the agent "Run this cron job now: Morning Weather"
- Verify Slack DM arrives with weather data

prompt:
## GitHub PR Review Monitor

### What It Does
An hourly weekday cron (7 AM – 4 PM PT) that checks GitHub for open PRs assigned to Adam for review. Only notifies via Slack DM when there are NEW unreviewed PRs.

### Architecture
- **Type:** OpenClaw cron job
- **Schedule:** `0 15,16,17,18,19,20,21,22,23,0 * * 1-5`

### Setup
1. Create OpenClaw cron:
   - Name: `🔍 GitHub PR Review Check`
   - Schedule: `0 15,16,17,18,19,20,21,22,23,0 * * 1-5`
   - Prompt: "Use gh CLI to check for open PRs assigned to me for review. If there are unreviewed PRs, send summary via Slack DM. If nothing new, reply NO_REPLY."

### Integration Points
- Uses `gh` CLI authenticated via GitHub OAuth
- Organization: `last-rev-llc`
- Also refreshes `data/prs.json` for the dashboard widget

### Testing
- Trigger: "Check for unreviewed PRs now"

name: Thread-First Communication
title: Thread-First Communication
description: Always respond in Slack threads. When replying in a thread, use that thread as the primary context.
prompt:
## Thread-First Communication Rule

### What It Does
A behavioral rule ensuring the agent always responds to Slack messages in a thread context, never as standalone channel messages.

### Implementation
- Always use `replyTo` parameter in message sends
- When user replies in a thread, treat that thread as the full context
- Read full thread via `message action=read` before responding

### Testing
- Send a message in a Slack channel
- Verify the agent responds in a thread

prompt:
## Command Center Password Gate

### What It Does
A localStorage-based password gate on all Command Center pages using the shared `<cc-auth>` Web Component.

### How It Works
1. `<cc-auth>` checks localStorage against configured password
2. If wrong: hides page content, shows login overlay
3. On correct: stores in localStorage, reveals page
4. `<cc-topbar>` Lock button clears auth and reloads

### Testing
- Open any page in incognito — verify login overlay
- Enter correct password — page reveals
- Navigate to another page — no re-auth required

name: App Database Skill
title: App Database Skill
description: Add SQLite database with server sync to any web app. Provides persistent data across browsers, devices, and incognito sessions using sql.js WASM + IndexedDB + server-side JSON sync.
prompt:
## App Database Skill

### What It Does
Adds a persistent SQLite database to any static web app. Data persists across browsers, devices, and incognito sessions using a three-layer architecture.

### Architecture
```
Browser (sql.js/WASM) ←→ Server (/api/sync/:file)
├── SQLite WASM — full SQL in browser
├── IndexedDB — local cache for fast loads
└── Server sync — /api/sync/:file.json reads/writes app data/ directory
```

### Setup
1. Add `sql-sync.js` script from shared library
2. Create `db.js` with schema config defining tables, columns, and JSON fields
3. Initialize SyncDB: `const db = await SyncDB.init(config)`
4. Use CRUD: `db.all()`, `db.get()`, `db.upsert()`, `db.remove()`
5. Sync: `db.pushAll()`, `db.pullAll()`

### Key Features
- Full SQL queries in browser via sql.js WASM
- Automatic IndexedDB caching for offline/fast loads
- Server-side JSON persistence with auto git commit
- Works across all app subdomains

### Testing
- Add data in one browser, verify it appears in another
- Clear localStorage, verify data recovers from server

## Idea Scoring Skill

### What It Does
The authoritative scoring rubric for all ideas. Every idea is scored using this system.

### Scoring Dimensions
- **Feasibility (1-10):** How realistic to build with current stack
  - 9-10: Trivial (<1 hour)
  - 7-8: Straightforward (1-2 days)
  - 5-6: Moderate (3-5 days)
  - 3-4: Challenging (1-2 weeks)
  - 1-2: Very difficult
- **Impact (1-10):** Value to Adam as COO
- **Effort:** Low (<1 day), Medium (1-3 days), High (>3 days)
- **Composite:** `round(feasibility * 0.3 + impact * 0.7, 1)`

### Feasibility Boosters
- Uses connected integrations (GitHub, Slack, Contentful, Google, etc.)
- Can be built as static app or Command Center module
- Leverages shared component library
- Similar to something already built

### Testing
- Score a new idea, verify dimensions are reasonable
- Check composite formula is applied correctly

prompt:
## CC Iron — Fitness Tracking App

### What It Does
A comprehensive fitness tracking application at https://cc-iron.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app featuring workout programs, exercise database, progress tracking, and personalized workout planning.

### Architecture
- **Tech stack:** Static HTML + vanilla JS + Supabase backend
- **File:** `apps/cc-iron/index.html` — tabbed single-page app
- **Database:** Supabase with tables for exercises, workouts, programs, progress
- **Shared components:** `<cc-topbar>`, `<cc-nav>`, `<cc-auth>`, custom cc-iron module
- **Files:** 
  - `db.js` — Supabase configuration
  - `create_tables.sql` — database schema
  - `seed_exercises.json` — exercise database
  - `seed_templates.json` — workout templates
  - `modules/cc-iron.js` — main app module

### Key Features
1. **Dashboard Tab** — overview of recent workouts, streak tracking, weekly goals
2. **Exercises Tab** — searchable database of exercises with:
   - Muscle group filtering
   - Exercise descriptions and form tips
   - Video/image references
3. **Workouts Tab** — workout logging with:
   - Set/rep/weight tracking
   - Rest timer functionality
   - Workout history
4. **Programs Tab** — structured workout programs:
   - Beginner to advanced progressions
   - Template customization
   - Weekly scheduling
5. **Progress Tab** — analytics and charts:
   - Strength progression graphs
   - Body measurements tracking
   - Goal setting and achievement

### Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE exercises (id, name, muscle_group, equipment, difficulty, instructions);
CREATE TABLE workouts (id, user_id, date, exercises_json, duration, notes);
CREATE TABLE programs (id, name, description, weeks, difficulty, workouts_json);
CREATE TABLE progress (id, user_id, date, metric, value, notes);
```

### Styling
- Orange accent theme (`--accent: #f97316`)
- Card-based layout with smooth transitions
- Mobile-responsive design
- Progress visualization with charts

### Testing
- Visit app, navigate between tabs
- Log a workout with exercises and weights
- Check progress tracking functionality
- Verify data persists across sessions

## Command Center App

The main Command Center dashboard at https://command-center.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app serving as the central hub with tabbed navigation to all productivity modules and sub-applications.

### Architecture
- **Tech stack:** Static HTML + vanilla JS + Supabase + SyncDB

- **Database:** `db.js` with SyncDB configuration for all data tables
- **Shared components:** `<cc-topbar>`, `<cc-nav>`, `<cc-auth>`, `<cc-toast>`

1. **Tabbed Interface** — Central navigation to:
   - **Overview** — summary widgets and quick stats
   - **Calendar** — Google Calendar integration
   - **PRs** — GitHub pull request tracking
   - **Slack** — message summaries and threads
   - **Ideas** — idea management and scoring

   - **Crons** — scheduled job monitoring
   - **Clients** — client health and relationship data

   - SyncDB for cross-device data synchronization
   - Supabase backend for persistent storage

3. **Progressive Web App:**

### File Structure

apps/command-center/
├── index.html (main dashboard)

prompt:
## Idea Capture Pipeline Skill

### What It Does
A comprehensive idea management skill that captures ideas via natural language, automatically categorizes and scores them, and integrates with the Ideas app for ongoing tracking.

### Architecture
- **Skill directory:** `skills/idea-capture-pipeline/`
- **Files:** `SKILL.md`, scoring algorithms, categorization logic
- **Integration:** Direct connection to `apps/command-center/data/ideas.json`

### Key Features
1. **Natural Language Capture** — 'log idea: [description]' in Slack
2. **Auto-Categorization** — Business, Technical, Product, Creative categories
3. **Smart Scoring:**
   - Feasibility (1-10) based on current capabilities
   - Impact (1-10) based on business value
   - Effort estimation (Low/Medium/High)
   - Composite score: `feasibility * 0.3 + impact * 0.7`
4. **Metadata Extraction:**
   - Similar solutions identification
   - Required integrations detection
   - Relevant tags generation
5. **Ideas App Integration** — seamless addition to browseable gallery

### Scoring Algorithm
- **Feasibility factors:** Available integrations, technical complexity, resource requirements
- **Impact factors:** Business value, user benefit, strategic alignment
- **Effort factors:** Development time, complexity, dependencies

### Usage Examples
- 'log idea: AI-powered client onboarding wizard'
- 'capture idea: Automated invoice reminder system'
- 'idea: Content calendar with AI topic suggestions'

### Output
Structured JSON added to ideas.json with all scoring and metadata populated

### Testing
- Capture various types of ideas
- Verify scoring accuracy and consistency
- Check integration with Ideas app display

prompt:
## Launch App Skill

### What It Does
Extends the create-app skill to launch full standalone SaaS applications with user authentication, Stripe billing, and per-app user management. Each app gets isolated users, profiles, and subscription data.

### Architecture
- **Skill directory:** `skills/launch-app/`
- **Files:** `SKILL.md`, auth setup scripts, billing integration helpers
- **Extends:** create-app skill with authentication and billing layers

### Key Features
1. **Authentication System:**
   - Supabase Auth integration
   - User registration and login flows
   - Email verification and password reset
   - Social login options (Google, GitHub)
2. **Billing Integration:**
   - Stripe subscription management
   - Multiple pricing tiers
   - Trial periods and prorations
   - Webhook handling for events
3. **User Management:**
   - Per-app user isolation
   - Profile management
   - Usage tracking and limits
   - Admin dashboard
4. **App Isolation:**
   - Separate user tables per app
   - Independent billing cycles
   - Custom domain support

### Setup Process
1. Create base app using create-app skill
2. Add Supabase Auth configuration
3. Set up Stripe products and pricing
4. Configure user onboarding flow
5. Deploy with authentication middleware

### Usage Examples
- 'Launch HSPTutor as a standalone SaaS'
- 'Add billing to the fitness tracker app'
- 'Create subscription tiers for the calculator'

### Integration Requirements
- Supabase project with Auth enabled
- Stripe account with configured products
- Custom domain for professional appearance

### Testing
- Complete user signup and login flow
- Process test subscription payments
- Verify user isolation between apps
